panels.description: Displays the total capacity, used storage, and usage percentage of the Alluxio cache. This panel is critical for monitoring overall cache utilization and preventing out-of-storage issues.
panels.title: Storage

panels.description: Shows the read throughput from different sources: Fuse, S3 API, and direct Worker access. This helps identify the primary data access interfaces for read operations.
panels.title: Read - Throughput

panels.description: Displays the average read load (thread utilization) across all workers over the last 5 minutes. A high average load may indicate a system-wide performance bottleneck.
panels.title: Read - Load (5m)

panels.description: Displays per-worker read load (for workers exceeding 50% utilization), and highlights the most heavily used workers. This helps identify uneven I/O distribution and potential hotspots within the cluster.
panels.title: Read - Hotspot (load > 50%)

panels.description: Displays the total number of files and pages currently stored in the Alluxio cache. This helps in understanding the composition and granularity of the cached data.
panels.title: Cache File

panels.description: Shows the write throughput from different sources: Fuse, S3 API, and direct Worker access. This helps identify the primary data access interfaces for write operations.
panels.title: Write - Throughput

panels.description: Displays the average write load (thread utilization) across all workers over the last 5 minutes. A high average load may indicate a system-wide performance bottleneck.
panels.title: Write - Load (5m)

panels.description: Displays per-worker write load (for workers exceeding 50% utilization), and highlights the most heavily used workers. This helps identify uneven I/O distribution and potential hotspots within the cluster.
panels.title: Write - Hotspot (load > 50%)

panels.description: Displays the number of live (healthy) and lost (unhealthy) workers in the cluster. This is a key indicator of cluster health and stability.
panels.title: Workers

panels.description: Displays the number of currently running and waiting jobs in the job service queue. This helps monitor the status of asynchronous operations like data loading.
panels.title: Jobs

panels.description: Displays the metadata Requests Per Second (RPS) from different sources: Fuse, S3 API, and direct Worker access. This is important for monitoring metadata workload intensity.
panels.title: Meta - RPS

panels.description: Displays the average metadata operation load (thread utilization) across all workers over the last 5 minutes. High load can indicate a metadata-intensive workload.
panels.title: Meta -  Load (5m)

panels.description: Displays per-worker metadata operation load (for workers exceeding 50% utilization), and highlights the most heavily used workers. This helps identify uneven I/O distribution and potential hotspots within the cluster.
panels.title: Meta - Hotspot (load > 50%)

panels.description: Displays the expiration date of the Alluxio enterprise license. This is important for ensuring uninterrupted access to enterprise features.
panels.title: License (Valid To)

panels.description: Displays the distribution of Alluxio versions running across the cluster. This is useful for verifying version consistency during upgrades or troubleshooting.
panels.title: Component Version

panels.description: Displays the utilization ratio of executor threads by opeartion types on workers, calculated as active threads over maximum threads.  The high average load may indicate a system-wide performance bottleneck.
panels.title: Cluster Load Trend

panels.description: Displays the uptime of each Alluxio component (coordinator, worker, fuse). This helps track service stability and identify recent restarts.
panels.title: Component Uptime

panels.description: Displays the percentage of data/metadata read requests served from the Alluxio cache. A higher value indicates better cache efficiency and reduced load on the underlying storage.
panels.title: Cache Hit(%)

panels.description: Displays the rate at which data is being evicted from the cache. A high eviction rate may indicate insufficient cache capacity for the workload.
panels.title: Cache Eviction

panels.description: Provides a detailed breakdown of read throughput across different data paths (FUSE, S3, Worker, UFS). Essential for understanding data flow and identifying bottlenecks.
panels.title: Throughput - Read

panels.description: Provides a detailed breakdown of write throughput across different data paths (FUSE, S3, Worker, UFS). Critical for understanding write efficiency and patterns.
panels.title: Throughput - Write

panels.description: Displays the total read request rate across all interfaces (FUSE, S3, Worker). Important for understanding read workload intensity and traffic patterns.
panels.title: Request/s - Read

panels.description: Displays the total write request rate across all interfaces. Critical for understanding write workload patterns and identifying write-heavy phases.

targets.expr: sum(sum(irate(alluxio_s3_api_throughput_bytes_count{method="write",job="worker",instance=~"$instance",cluster_name=~"$cluster"}[5m])) or on() vector(0)) or on() vector(0)

panels.title: Request/s - Write

panels.description: Displays the metadata operation request rate across all interfaces. Important for understanding metadata workload intensity, which can impact overall cluster performance.
panels.title: Request/s - Metadata

panels.description: Time series visualization of job execution patterns. Important for understanding cluster background activity levels and identifying periods of high maintenance activity that might impact performance.
panels.title: Jobs

panels.description: Displays the 90th percentile latency for all request types. This metric is key to understanding the typical user experience and identifying performance degradation.
panels.title: Request Latency - P90

panels.description: Displays the 99th percentile latency for all request types. This metric is critical for identifying worst-case performance and outliers that may indicate system stress.
panels.title: Request Latency - P99

panels.description: Displays the estimated network latency for client GetStatus gRPC calls (metadata operations), calculated as the difference between client-observed latency and worker metadata processing latency. This helps identify network-related delays and connectivity issues between clients and the cluster.
panels.title: RPC (Client<->Worker) Latency - P90

panels.description: Displays the estimated network latency for client GetStatus gRPC calls (metadata operations), calculated as the difference between client-observed latency and worker metadata processing latency. This helps identify network-related delays and connectivity issues between clients and the cluster.
panels.title: RPC (Client<->Worker) Latency  - P99

panels.description: Displays the success rate for all request types across interfaces. A value below 99% (excluding Not Found errors) typically indicates system issues that require investigation.
panels.title: Request Call - Success Rate

panels.description: Displays the failure rate across all request types and interfaces. Spikes in this metric require immediate investigation to identify system or application errors.
panels.title: Request Call - Failures

panels.description: Displays the 90th percentile latency for individual Fuse mount operations. This helps isolate performance issues specific to a particular Fuse client.
panels.title: Request Latency - P90

panels.description: Displays the 99th percentile latency for individual Fuse mount operations. This helps isolate performance issues specific to a particular Fuse client.
panels.title: Request Latency - P99

panels.description: Displays the read throughput for an individual Fuse mount. This is useful for monitoring the read activity of a specific client.
panels.title: Throughput - Read

panels.description: Displays the write throughput for an individual Fuse mount. This is useful for monitoring the write activity of a specific client.
panels.title: Throughput - Write

panels.description: Displays the read request rate for an individual Fuse mount. This helps understand the read workload from a specific client.
panels.title: Request/s - Read

panels.description: Displays the write request rate for an individual Fuse mount. This helps understand the write workload from a specific client.
panels.title: Request/s - Write

panels.description: Displays the failure rate for an individual Fuse mount. This is critical for diagnosing client-specific connectivity or operational issues.
panels.title: FUSE Request Failure

panels.description: Read throughput when FUSE operations fall back to underlying filesystem (UFS). High fallback rates indicate cache misses or data not in Alluxio, reducing performance benefits. Should be minimal for optimal performance.
panels.title: Throughput - UFS Fallback

panels.description: Displays the latency distribution of gRPC (metadata ops) and Netty (data ops) client calls. This helps identify RPC communication delays, client-side bottlenecks, and potential network or service responsiveness issues within the workers.
panels.title: Client Request Latency (P99)

panels.description: Displays the number of concurrent gRPC client calls (metadata ops). This helps identify spikes in metadata request load and potential client-side bottlenecks within the worker.
panels.title: Client Request Concurrency

panels.description: Displays the error rate of gRPC client calls (metadata operations) and Netty operation calls (data operations). This helps identify failing metadata requests and potential reliability issues in client-worker communication within the cluster.
panels.title: Client Request Failure Rate

panels.description: Displays the total number of gRPC client errors (metadata operations) and Netty operation errors (data operations). This helps track failing requests and potential reliability issues in client-worker communication within the cluster.
panels.title: Client Request Errors

panels.description: Displays the 90th percentile latency for S3 API operations on a per-worker basis. This helps isolate S3 performance issues to specific workers.
panels.title: Request Latency - P90

panels.description: Displays the 99th percentile latency for S3 API operations on a per-worker basis. This helps identify worst-case S3 performance on specific workers.
panels.title: Request Latency - P99

panels.description: Displays the read throughput for S3 API operations on a per-worker basis. This is useful for monitoring S3 read activity on specific workers.
panels.title: Throughput - Read

panels.description: Displays the write throughput for S3 API operations on a per-worker basis. This is useful for monitoring S3 write activity on specific workers.
panels.title: Throughput - Write

panels.description: Displays the read request rate for S3 API operations on a per-worker basis. This helps understand the S3 read workload on specific workers.
panels.title: Request/s - Read

panels.description: Displays the write request rate for S3 API operations on a per-worker basis. This helps understand the S3 write workload on specific workers.
panels.title: Request/s - Write

panels.description: Displays the failure rate for S3 API operations on a per-worker basis. This is critical for diagnosing S3-specific issues on individual workers.
panels.title: Request Failure

panels.description: Displays the amount of cache data storage used by each individual worker. This is useful for identifying uneven storage distribution.
panels.title: Storage (Data) Used

panels.description: Displays the amount of cache metadata storage used by each individual worker. This is useful for identifying uneven storage distribution.
panels.title: Storage (Meta)  Used

panels.description: Displays the page store device capacity utilization per directory on workers. This helps identify disk space consumption trends and potential storage exhaustion or imbalance within the cluster.
panels.title: Storage Device Usage

panels.description: Displays the number of cached files for each individual worker. This helps in understanding the cache composition on a per-worker basis.
panels.title: Files

panels.description: Displays the number of cached pages for each individual worker. This helps in understanding the cache composition on a per-worker basis.
panels.title: Pages

panels.description: Displays the rate of data eviction from the cache for each individual worker. This helps identify which workers are under the most memory pressure.
panels.title: Cache Evicted

panels.description: Displays the 90th percentile latency for internal metadata operations on a per-worker basis.
panels.title: Request Latency - P90

panels.description: Displays the 99th percentile latency for internal metadata operations on a per-worker basis.
panels.title: Request Latency - P99

panels.description: Displays the latency distribution for read operations served by Alluxio workers from the page store or underlying file system (UFS). This helps identify read performance bottlenecks and distinguish slow storage backends or overloaded workers within the cluster.
panels.title: Request (from Page Store or UFS) Latency - P90

panels.description: Displays the 90th percentile latency of page store I/O operations (data operations) on workers. This helps identify slow disk I/O performance and potential storage bottlenecks within the cluster.
panels.title: PageStore IO Latency - P90

panels.description: Displays the read throughput for each individual worker. This is useful for monitoring the read activity and load on each worker.
panels.title: Read - Throughput

panels.description: Displays the write throughput for each individual worker. This is useful for monitoring the write activity and load on each worker.
panels.title: Write - Throughput

panels.description: Displays the read request rate for each individual worker. This helps understand the read workload distribution across workers.
panels.title: Read - Request/s

panels.description: Displays the write request rate for each individual worker. This helps understand the write workload distribution across workers.
panels.title: Write - Request/s

panels.description: Displays the active threads and queue length for the read thread pool on each worker. This is useful for diagnosing read performance bottlenecks.
panels.title: Read - Threads

panels.description: Displays the active threads and queue length for the write thread pool on each worker. This is useful for diagnosing write performance bottlenecks.
panels.title: Write - Threads

panels.description: Displays the metadata request rate for each individual worker. This helps identify workers with high metadata load.
panels.title: Metadata - Request/s

panels.description: Displays the active threads and queue length for the metadata thread pool on each worker. This is useful for diagnosing metadata performance bottlenecks.
panels.title: Metadata - Threads

panels.description: Displays the failure rate for internal metadata operations on a per-worker basis. This helps pinpoint workers that are experiencing internal errors.
panels.title: Request Failure

panels.description: Displays the off-heap memory usage for each worker. This is important for monitoring memory resources and preventing out-of-memory errors.
panels.title: Off Heap Memory

panels.description: Displays the error rate for PageStore operations on each worker. This can indicate issues with the local cache storage layer.
panels.title: PageStore Errors

panels.description: Displays the error rate for disk-related PageStore operations on each worker. This can indicate underlying disk hardware or file system issues.

targets.expr:
sum by (instance, dir) (irate(alluxio_page_store_dir_operation_errors_total{job="worker", cluster_name=~"$cluster", instance=~"$instance"}[5m]) or on() vector(0))
/
sum by (instance, dir) (irate(alluxio_page_store_dir_operations_total{job="worker", cluster_name=~"$cluster", instance=~"$instance"}[5m]))

panels.title: PageStore Disk Error Rate

panels.description: Displays the 90th percentile latency for UFS operations on a per-worker basis. This helps identify slow UFS interactions from specific workers.
panels.title: Request Latency - P90

panels.description: Displays the 99th percentile latency for UFS operations on a per-worker basis. This helps identify worst-case UFS performance from specific workers.
panels.title: Request Latency - P99

panels.description: Displays the read throughput from the UFS on a per-worker basis. This is useful for monitoring how much data is being read from the underlying storage.
panels.title: Throughput - Read

panels.description: Displays the write throughput to the UFS on a per-worker basis. This is useful for monitoring how much data is being written to the underlying storage.
panels.title: Throughput - Write

panels.description: Displays the error rate for UFS operations on a per-worker basis. This is critical for diagnosing issues with the underlying storage system.
panels.title: Errors

panels.description: Time series visualization of job execution patterns. Important for understanding cluster background activity levels and identifying periods of high maintenance activity that might impact performance.
panels.title: Jobs

panels.description: Time series visualization of job execution tasks on each worker. Important for understanding worker activity levels and identifying bottlenecks that might impact performance.
panels.title: Job Tasks per Worker

panels.description: Displays the active threads and queue length for the load job thread pool on each worker. This is useful for diagnosing load job performance bottlenecks.
panels.title: Job Threads per Worker

panels.description: Displays the rate of jobs dispatched per worker.
panels.title: Job Dispatched Per Second

panels.description: Displays the load job throughput for each individual worker. This is useful for monitoring the load job activity on each worker.
panels.title: Distributed Load  Throughput

panels.description: Displays the rate of bytes loaded on workers.
panels.title: Bytes Loaded on Workers Per Second

panels.description: Displays the rate of distributed load operations.
panels.title: Distributed Load Operation Counts Per Second

panels.description: Displays the failure breakdown by reason and worker.
panels.title: Distributed Load Failure Breakdowns

panels.description: Displays the rate of bytes written from clients to the write cache per worker. This helps monitor client write throughput and identify imbalances or potential bottlenecks in the write cache ingestion path.
panels.title: Client to Write Cache Throughput

panels.description: Displays the rate of bytes persisted asynchronously from the write cache to UFS. This helps monitor write cache flush throughput and identify potential bottlenecks in persisting data to underlying storage.
panels.title: Write Cache to UFS (Async Persist) Throughput

panels.description: Displays the number of running and waiting write cache persist tasks (write cache operations) on workers. This helps identify write cache backlog, persist throughput limitations, and potential pressure on underlying storage within the cluster.
panels.title: Async Persist Task In Queue

panels.description: Displays the rate of completed or failed write cache persist tasks on workers (write cache operations). This helps track write cache flush progress and identify persist failures or throughput issues within the cluster.
panels.title: Async Persist Task

panels.description: Displays the average latency of write cache metadata operations (FoundationDB-based), aggregated by method. This helps identify slow write cache metadata operations and potential performance bottlenecks in FoundationDB interactions within the cluster.
panels.title: Metadata Operation Latency

panels.description: Displays the total number of write cache transitions where the worker acted as a follower. This helps track the distribution of write cache coordination roles and identify potential imbalances or hotspots within the cluster.
panels.title: Async Persist Transition Follower Times per Worker

panels.description: Displays the rate of failed write cache metadata operations (FoundationDB-based) on workers. This helps identify failing metadata interactions and potential reliability issues in write cache operations within the cluster.
panels.title: Metadata Operation Failures

panels.description: Displays the rate of successful write cache metadata operations (FoundationDB-based) on workers. This helps track successful metadata interactions and monitor the throughput of write cache operations within the cluster.
panels.title: Metadata Operation Success

panels.description: Displays the total count of write operations in the write cache, categorized by status (e.g., success, failure). This helps track write cache operation outcomes and identify potential reliability or error trends within the cluster.
panels.title: Write Status

panels.description: Displays the rate of write cache operation failures per worker. This helps identify failing write cache operations and potential reliability issues within the cluster.
panels.title: Write Failure

panels.description: Displays the rate of unique bytes written to the write cache across all workers. This helps monitor overall write cache ingestion and identify trends in data write volume within the cluster.
panels.title: Unique Write Throughput

panels.description: write cache ingestion and identify imbalances or hotspots in data write activity within the cluster.
panels.title: Unique Write Throughput per Worker

panels.description: Displays the rate of file system requests handled by the dual buffer on workers. This helps monitor dual buffer activity and identify potential bottlenecks or high request load within the cluster.
panels.title: Dual FileSystem Request

panels.description: Displays the total number of worker membership refreshes on each instance. This helps track cluster topology updates and potential instability or frequent reconfiguration of workers within the cluster.
panels.title: Active Worker Membership

panels.description: Displays the utilization ratio of dynamic resource pools, calculated as current resources over maximum capacity. This helps identify over- or under-utilized pools and potential resource bottlenecks within the cluster.

targets.expr:
sum by (instance, pool_kind, pool_instance) (alluxio_dynamic_resource_pool_current_resources{job=~"$service",instance=~"$instance",cluster_name=~"$cluster"})
/
sum by (instance, pool_kind, pool_instance) (alluxio_dynamic_resource_pool_capacity{type="max",job=~"$service",instance=~"$instance",cluster_name=~"$cluster"})

panels.title: Resource Pool Usage

panels.description: Displays the rate of dynamic resource pool acquisition timeouts. This helps identify contention or delays in resource allocation and potential performance bottlenecks within the cluster.
panels.title: Resource Pool - Acquisition Timeout

panels.description: Displays the 99th percentile latency for creating new resources in dynamic resource pools. This helps identify slow resource allocation and potential performance bottlenecks within the cluster.
panels.title: Resource Pool - Resource Creation Latency (P99)

panels.description: Displays the CPU time spent by each process.
panels.title: CPU time spent

panels.description: Displays the number of threads for each process.
panels.title: Threads

panels.description: Displays the heap memory usage for each process.
panels.title: Heap Usage

panels.description: Displays the heap memory usage percentage for each process.
panels.title: Heap Usage(%)

panels.description: Displays the young generation garbage collection time per minute.
panels.title: young GC time(per minute)

panels.description: Displays the young generation garbage collection rate per minute.
panels.title: young GC rate(per minute)

panels.description: Displays the old generation garbage collection time per minute.
panels.title: old GC time(per minute)

panels.description: Displays the old generation garbage collection rate per minute.
panels.title: old GC rate(per minute)

panels.description: Displays the number of node in ETCD cluster.
panels.title: Up

panels.description: Displays the RPC request and failure rates.
panels.title: RPC Rate

panels.description: Displays the number of active watch and lease streams.
panels.title: Active Streams

panels.description: Displays the error rate of Etcd client calls. This helps identify failing Etcd requests and potential reliability or connectivity issues between the cluster and the Etcd service.

targets.expr:
(sum by (instance, server) (irate(alluxio_etcd_call_errors_total{job=~"$service",instance=~"$instance",cluster_name=~"$cluster"}[5m])) or on() vector(0))
/
(sum by (instance, server) (irate(alluxio_etcd_client_calls_total{job=~"$service",instance=~"$instance",cluster_name=~"$cluster"}[5m])))

panels.title: ETCD Client Call Failure Rate

panels.description: Displays the 99th percentile latency of Etcd client calls. This helps identify slow Etcd operations and potential performance bottlenecks in Etcd communication within the cluster.
panels.title: ETCD Client Call Latency (P99)

panels.description: Displays the size of the ETCD database.
panels.title: DB Size

panels.description: Displays the disk sync duration for WAL and backend operations.
panels.title: Disk Sync Duration

panels.description: Displays the resident memory usage of the ETCD process.
panels.title: Memory

panels.description: Displays the client traffic in rates.
panels.title: Client Traffic In

panels.description: Displays the client traffic out rates.
panels.title: Client Traffic Out

panels.description: Displays the peer traffic in rates.
panels.title: Peer Traffic In

panels.description: Displays the peer traffic out rates.
panels.title: Peer Traffic Out

panels.description: Displays the raft proposal metrics including failure rate, pending total, commit rate, and apply rate.
panels.title: Raft Proposals

panels.description: Displays the total number of leader elections per day.
panels.title: Total Leader Elections Per Day
